title: Experience contract
description: An executable, per-surface contract that sits between the spec and the plan in feature-development. It locks one user- or system-facing surface (screen/flow/api/job/cli/data/integration) in enough verifiable detail that planning, tasks, implementation, and verify can enforce it. The companion to the markdown experience doc; this JSON is the machine-checkable half. Lives at projects/<project>/specs/<spec>/experience/<surface>.contract.json.

schemaVersion.description: Semantic version of this contract shape.

spec.description: The owning spec id (matches the spec folder).

surface.description: Surface slug. Matches the experience markdown file stem and the plan/tasks surface group.

experienceType.description: What kind of surface this is. Drives which verification gates apply.

route.description: Route, endpoint, command invocation, job trigger, or entry point for the surface.

sourceOfTruth.description: The authoritative references the surface is built from (Figma node, API doc, schema, fixture, screenshot). Every one must be reachable or committed before implementation, or stop condition U-1 fires.

kind.description: figma | api-doc | schema | fixture | screenshot | doc | other.

committedPath.description: Repo-relative path to a committed copy/snapshot, when applicable.

filesOwned.description: Paths this surface owns, scoping the verifier and the tasks' allowed edit surface. Cross-cutting files belong to scaffold/cross-cutting, not here. Empty ownership means verification cannot scope this surface.

components.description: Dependency map: every named primitive, module, endpoint, or dependency must resolve to an existing source or an explicitly planned new local artifact. A named substitution at implementation time is stop condition U-9.

verification.description: How this surface is proven. Each entry names a gate (from the workflow gate catalog), the evidence that runs it, and the pass criteria. A surface that cannot be verified must not be built (stop condition U-7).

gate.description: Gate id from the workflow gate catalog, e.g. G-build, G-routes-200.

evidence.description: The command, test, or manual protocol that produces the result.

status.description: Lifecycle, from a closed set. 'accepted' is what authorizes the plan step for this surface. Pending is not approval.

openQuestions.description: Unresolved questions. A non-empty list gates the surface: it cannot be 'accepted' until cleared or explicitly deferred by a decision.
